column4: Explanation
This indicates if you want to manually add all the initializtion parameters. Leave it as random.
Number of nodes that we want in the graph
The way we want to generate the graph. The values can be erdos_renyi (p), barabasi_albert (m), watts_strogatz (k, p), balance_tree (r, h)
column5: The Barabasi-Albert (BA) model generates "scale-free" networks, which are the most realistic representation of the broad Computing Continuum.
column6:
Naryshkin, A. A., et al. (2021). "Monte Carlo Approach to the Computational Capacities Analysis of the Computing Continuum." International Conference on Computational Science — monte_carlo_approach.pdf (page 5)
Massa, J., et al. (2024). "Evaluation and Efficiency Comparison of Evolutionary Algorithms for Service Placement Optimization in Fog Architectures." arXiv preprint. —

column4: Parameter of the Barabasi Albert model
Defines how often you want to disable a random node
When we call disable_random_node it automatically adds an event to revive the node and in here we defines how often we want to revive that node that we have disabled
Defines how often you want to disable a random edge
When we call disable_random_edge it automatically adds an event to revive the edge and in here we defines how often we want to revive that edge that we have disabled
RAM of the node
column5: We make sure that the nodes that have the biggest RAM act like "hubs".  We keep {num_nodes} so the parser can format it via kwargs.

column4: Every time an app gets created at the beginning of the simualtion a random amount of users (between one and six) get associated to this app

column5: This value is not used anymore

The inter-arrival time between consecutive app requests from a single user follows an exponential distribution. You would assign a rate parameter lambda (e.g., 5 requests per minute) to generate these temporal timestamps in your simulation.
column6: Huang, S., Lv, B., Wang, R., & Huang, K. (2020). Scheduling for mobile edge computing with random user arrivals: An approximate MDP and reinforcement learning approach. arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2004.07553 - huang-pdf

column4: Users stay for 300 time units on average
column5: The time a user spends in either state (new or remove) before transitioning to the other is modeled using an exponential distribution. On p. 7 of the paper: “we considered applications with a mean active/sleeping time (1/λ) of 300 seconds”
column6: Donassolo, B., Legrand, A., Mertikopoulos, P., & Fajjari, I. (2022). Online reconfiguration of IoT applications in the fog: The information-coordination trade-off. IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, 33, 1156–1172. https://doi.org/10.1109/tpds.2021.3097281 - donassolo.pdf page 7

column4: Users move approximately every 120 units of time
column5: In Random Waypoint (RWP) mobility model, a user randomly selects a destination node in the network and moves toward it at a speed chosen uniformly from a predefined range (e.g., a uniform distribution between 0 and 50 m/s).
column6: Zhang, Y., Liang, W., Xu, Z., & Jia, X. (2024). Mobility-aware service provisioning in edge computing via digital twin replica placements. IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing, 23, 11295–11311. https://doi.org/10.1109/tmc.2024.3394839
